# Daily Lift — v1 Test Plan

**Generated by:** Claude Sonnet 4.6 (`claude-sonnet-4-6`)
**Tokens used to produce this document (measured):** ~590,500 — measured from
this session's transcript (`cache_creation_input_tokens +
cache_read_input_tokens + output_tokens`, summed across the API calls covering
this request, deduplicated where one call emitted multiple log lines). This
figure covers the combined work that produced this document **and** the
testing-approach revisions to `build-plan-hardened.md`, written in the same
response — they share the same accumulated context and can't be cleanly
separated. As with prior figures in this project, most of this total is
`cache_read_input_tokens` (re-processing accumulated context as the
conversation grows), billed at a steep discount vs. fresh input. No cost
figure included, per your request.

---

## Purpose

This is the complete list of test cases for the v1 build, derived from
`build-plan-hardened.md` and `DECISION-LOG.md`. Every test case is marked:

- **Automated (Claude)** — Claude Code writes this test (JUnit unit test or
  Compose UI test), runs it via Gradle, and **fixes any failure itself**
  before pausing for you. You don't need to do anything for these — they're
  listed here so you can see what's already been verified.
- **Manual (PM)** — requires a human: a visual/tone judgment call, a change
  to your phone's Settings (Claude can't do this for you), or real-device
  timing. Claude will give you exact steps to follow at the relevant point in
  the build.

The goal is to make the **Manual** list as short as possible. As of this
plan, **34 of 46 test cases (74%) are automated** — see the Summary at the
bottom for the full breakdown.

---

## Step A — Environment setup + blank app

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| A1 | `./gradlew build` completes with no compile errors | Automated (Claude) |
| A2 | Compose UI test confirms the "Hello Daily Lift" text renders on launch | Automated (Claude) |
| A3 | Android Studio, SDK, and an emulator or physical device are installed and working on your machine | Manual (PM) |
| A4 | Launch the app yourself once and confirm you see "Hello Daily Lift" | Manual (PM) |

---

## Step B — Data model + seed data + local persistence

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| B1 | JSON round-trip (serialize → deserialize) for `Exercise`, `WorkoutDay`, `WorkoutData`, and `Completion` produces identical data | Automated (Claude) |
| B2 | Seed data contains no literal "AMRAP" string anywhere in `reps` (Decision 2 fix) | Automated (Claude) |
| B3 | Every seeded exercise has a unique, non-empty `id` (Decision 3) | Automated (Claude) |
| B4 | Seed data covers Monday–Friday only; `WorkoutData` has no Saturday/Sunday entries | Automated (Claude) |
| B5 | Every seeded exercise has non-empty `imageStartRef` and `imageEndRef` values (final art wired in Step E; Decision 4) | Automated (Claude) |
| B6 | Save data, simulate an app restart (reload from the JSON file), confirm reloaded data matches what was saved | Automated (Claude) |
| B7 | *(Optional)* Nothing to check yet — Step B has no new UI. If you're curious, you can close and reopen the app; it should still show the Step A screen with no errors | Manual (PM, optional) |

---

## Step C — Main "today" screen + day navigation (read-only)

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| C1 | Unit test: each weekday Monday–Friday maps to its correct seeded workout (focus label + exercise list) | Automated (Claude) |
| C2 | Unit test: Saturday and Sunday both map to the "rest day" state | Automated (Claude) |
| C3 | Unit test (mocked clock): if the date changes while the app is backgrounded, resuming the app recomputes "today" and refreshes the Today badge | Automated (Claude) |
| C4 | Compose UI test: left/right day-navigation arrows cycle through all 7 days, each showing its correct focus label | Automated (Claude) |
| C5 | Compose UI test: the static "Do 3 sets of each exercise below" line renders once per workout-day view (Decision 2) | Automated (Claude) |
| C6 | Compose UI test: Saturday/Sunday show the "Rest & recover" message plus a "Next up" preview of Monday's first exercises | Automated (Claude) |
| C7 | Automated contrast check: column header colors and "Rest Day"/"Next up" label colors compute to ≥4.5:1 against the card background (Decision 6) | Automated (Claude) |
| C8 | Unit test: viewing a non-today day always displays its exercises as unchecked, regardless of stored completion state | Automated (Claude) |
| MC1 | Open the app and confirm today's workout content and the feel of day-navigation look right | Manual (PM) |
| MC2 | If today is a weekday, navigate to Saturday/Sunday and confirm the rest-day screen and "Next up" preview look right | Manual (PM) |

---

## Step D — Editing

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| D1 | Unit test: checking off an exercise updates the completion map keyed by `id` (not position); unchecking reverses it | Automated (Claude) |
| D2 | Unit test: Bodyweight toggle ON sets `weight = "bodyweight"` and hides the numeric input; toggle OFF restores numeric entry (Decision 5, edge case #2) | Automated (Claude) |
| D3 | Unit test: weight display logic — `""` → "—", `"bodyweight"` → "Body" (no unit suffix), numeric → "{value} lb" | Automated (Claude) |
| D4 | Unit test: rename input enforces the ~30-character cap (Decision 5, edge case #3) | Automated (Claude) |
| D5 | Unit test: "Add exercise" generates a new unique `id`, the default tip text, and the generic placeholder `imageStartRef`/`imageEndRef` pair (Decision 5, edge case #9) | Automated (Claude) |
| D6 | Unit test: deleting an exercise removes it and its `id`'s entry from the completion map, with no index corruption to remaining exercises (Decision 3) | Automated (Claude) |
| D7 | Compose UI test: deleting an exercise shows a confirm dialog before it's removed | Automated (Claude) |
| D8 | Compose UI test: a workout day with zero exercises shows "Rest Day" in place of the list, with "Add exercise" still visible (Decision 5, edge case #1) | Automated (Claude) |
| D9 | Compose UI test: checkbox, day-nav arrows, pencil, trash, and the Bodyweight toggle each measure ≥48x48dp tappable area (Decision 6) | Automated (Claude) |
| D10 | Compose UI/semantics test: icon-only controls expose a `contentDescription` including current state (e.g., "Mark Goblet squat done" / "Goblet squat, marked done", "Bodyweight toggle, on") | Automated (Claude) |
| D11 | Unit test: the "—" empty-weight indicator has a spoken accessibility label ("no weight set"), not a literal dash | Automated (Claude) |
| D12 | Unit test (mocked clock): completion map resets to empty when the stored date differs from today | Automated (Claude) |
| MD1 | In your phone's Settings, set system font size to 130%. Reopen the app and confirm rows wrap to two lines (name on top; weight/reps/toggle/icons below) instead of clipping or truncating | Manual (PM) — requires a device Settings change |
| MD2 | Try editing weight/reps, toggling Bodyweight, renaming, adding, and deleting an exercise yourself — confirm it feels intuitive | Manual (PM) |
| MD3 | *(Optional)* Turn on TalkBack and spot-check that a couple of icon-only controls announce sensibly | Manual (PM, optional) |
| MD4 | Check off a few exercises, then either wait until after midnight or move your device's date forward a day, reopen the app, and confirm completion resets and "Today" updates | Manual (PM) — requires real-device clock/time |

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| D1.1 | Designer reviews the full Step D editing UI on the emulator and documents specific observations (Bodyweight toggle UX, 130%-font-scale layout, and any other issues) in `design-review.md` | Manual (Designer) |
| D1.2 | PM reviews the designer's feedback in `design-review.md` and adds implementation direction for each item before Step D.2 begins | Manual (PM) |

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| D2.1 | `./gradlew test connectedDebugAndroidTest lintDebug` passes with 0 failures after all D.2 UI changes (re-verifies D1–D12 + lint baseline) | Automated (Claude) |

## Step E — Detail view + visuals + all-done message

**Image generation deferred (DECISION-LOG.md Addendum 10):** no image-generation tool is
available to Claude in this environment, so E6/ME1/ME2 below are adapted or deferred — see notes
on each. Everything else (detail view, navigation, all-done message) is built and tested in full.

| ID | Test case | Type |
|---|---|---|
| E1 | Unit test: the detail view resolves the correct `imageStartRef`/`imageEndRef`/tip for a seeded exercise | Automated (Claude) — `SeedWorkoutDataTest.seededExerciseResolvesToItsOwnImageRefsAndTip`, `.repeatedExerciseNameAcrossDaysResolvesToTheSameImageRefs` |
| E2 | Unit test: a custom/added exercise resolves to the generic placeholder image pair (Decision 5, edge case #9) | Automated (Claude) — covered by the existing D5 test, `TodayViewModelTest.addExerciseAppendsNewPlaceholderExerciseWithUniqueIdAndDefaults` |
| E3 | Unit test: the all-done message triggers when every exercise for today is checked AND today's list is non-empty | Automated (Claude) — `TodayUiStateTest.isAllDoneTrueWhenEveryExerciseForTodayIsChecked`, `.isAllDoneFalseWhenAnyExerciseForTodayIsUnchecked`; UI-level: `TodayScreenEditingTest.allDoneBannerAppearsOnlyWhenEveryExerciseIsChecked` |
| E4 | Unit test: the all-done message does NOT trigger on a Rest Day (Sat/Sun) or on an empty weekday showing "Rest Day" | Automated (Claude) — `TodayUiStateTest.isAllDoneFalseOnRestDay`, `.isAllDoneFalseOnEmptyWorkoutDay` |
| E5 | Compose UI test: detail view renders name, weight/reps line, both images labeled "Start"/"End", and the form tip | Automated (Claude) — `ExerciseDetailScreenTest.detailViewRendersNameWeightRepsImagesAndTip` |
| E6 | **Adapted** — no real drawable resources exist yet, so this can't test "resolves to an image resource that exists." Adapted to: every seeded exercise (+ the placeholder pair) has well-formed, correctly-paired `imageStartRef`/`imageEndRef` strings ready to wire in once artwork exists. | Automated (Claude) — same tests as E1 |
| ME1 | **Deferred.** Was: required gate to review one sample image before bulk-generating the rest. No images exist yet to review — deferred until image generation happens (Addendum 10). | Manual (PM) — deferred |
| ME2 | **Deferred.** Was: open detail views and confirm images/tips feel right. Tips can be checked now; images are placeholders until artwork exists (Addendum 10). | Manual (PM) — deferred for images, open for tips |
| ME3 | Complete every exercise for today and confirm the all-done celebration message displays and feels right | Manual (PM) |

## Summary

| Step | Automated (Claude) | Manual (PM / Designer) | Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | 2 | 2 | 4 |
| B | 6 | 1 (optional) | 7 |
| C | 8 | 2 | 10 |
| D | 12 | 4 (1 optional) | 16 |
| D.1 | 0 | 2 (D1.1 = Designer, D1.2 = PM) | 2 |
| D.2 | 1 | 1 | 2 |
| E | 6 | 3 | 9 |
| **Total** | **35** | **15** | **50** |

**70% of all test cases are run and fixed by Claude before you're asked to do
anything.** Of the 15 manual items, only 4 are required at a specific point
in the build with no automated alternative possible:

- **A3/A4** — your machine's dev environment setup, and confirming the app
  launches on your device (Claude can guide you, but can't act on your
  machine).
- **MD1** — the 130% font-scale check requires changing your phone's
  Settings, which Claude cannot do remotely.
- **ME1** — the one-image style-approval gate is a deliberate human checkpoint
  (per your direction) — a judgment call only you can make.

The rest (MC1, MC2, MD2, ME2, ME3) are quick "does this feel right" passes —
recommended, but lower-stakes than the four above. B7 and MD3 are optional.

---

## Recommendation: which Claude model to use for this build

**Currently in use: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (`claude-sonnet-4-6`).** This is *not*
the most capable model available — that's **Claude Opus 4.8**
(`claude-opus-4-8`), the top of the current Claude 4.x lineup. There's also
**Claude Haiku 4.5** (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`), a smaller/faster model.

**Recommendation: stay on Sonnet 4.6 for the bulk of this build.** Most of
Steps A–E is well-specified implementation work — Android/Kotlin/Compose
code, JSON persistence, and the JUnit/Compose UI tests above — which Sonnet
handles reliably and more efficiently (lower token usage per step, which
matters since this session already tracks token usage closely). Opus's extra
reasoning depth is most valuable for *ambiguous, high-stakes* decisions, not
routine implementation.

**Two situations where switching to Opus is worth it:**

1. **If something unexpected comes up during Step B** (the data model). This
   is the foundation everything else builds on — a subtle mistake here is
   expensive to unwind later (it's exactly the kind of thing Decision 3,
   stable IDs, was designed to get right up front). If Claude flags any
   ambiguity in the data model during Step B, it's worth the extra reasoning
   depth.
2. **If Claude gets stuck** — e.g., a test keeps failing after 2-3 fix
   attempts, or a build error resists diagnosis. Opus is the better fallback
   for hard debugging.

**How to switch:** Claude Code's `/fast` toggle switches to Opus (with faster
output) and can be turned on/off per step with no setup cost — so if you
want extra confidence on Step B specifically, you can toggle it on for that
step and back off afterward.

**Haiku 4.5 is not recommended for this build** — it's a faster/cheaper model
better suited to trivial, high-volume tasks, not a multi-step app build with
real architectural decisions.
